texto: Computers and computing are all around us. Some computing is highly visible, like your laptop. But this is only part of a computing iceberg. A lot more lies hidden below the surface. We don't see and usually don't think about the computers inside appliances, cars, airplanes, cameras, smartphones, GPS navigators and games.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Brian Kernighan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Computers and computing are all around us. Some computing is highly visible, like your laptop. Bu…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, think, hidden pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de hidden e see.

frase_original: Computers and computing are all around us. Some computing is highly visible, like your laptop. But this is only part of a computing iceberg. A lot more lies hidden below the surface. We don't see and usually don't think about the computers inside appliances, cars, airplanes, cameras, smartphones, GPS navigators and games.

texto: Even though most people won't be directly involved with programming, everyone is affected by computers, so an educated person should have a good understanding of how computer hardware, software, and networks operate.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Brian Kernighan sobre temas ligados a trabalho. A afirmação “Even though most people won't be directly involved with programming, everyone is affected by comp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a trabalho, good, people, útil em momentos de trabalho e people.

texto: It's important to be informed about issues like usability, reliability, security, privacy, and some of the inherent limitations of computers.
